title: 'Operation Pushkin': Rare books, Georgian con artists and the shadow of the Kremlin
subhed: For two years, Georgian criminals orchestrated the theft of rare editions of works by Alexander Pushkin from major European libraries. The trial of five of them begins in Paris on June 9. It could reveal whether Russia was involved.

'The SpaceX stock market debut reads more like science fiction than a realistic business plan'
subhed: Elon Musk is basing the market valuation of his aerospace company, which is set to go public on the Nasdaq on Friday, on the promise of dominating technologies and markets that do not yet exist. In the space sector, however, the line between achievement and disaster is razor-thin, explains Le Monde columnist Stéphane Lauer.

title: Paris Mayor Grégoire confronts the dark side of his predecessor's legacy
subhed: Sexual violence in after-school programs has overshadowed the start of the new mayor's term, as he must bear the legacy of Anne Hidalgo, whom he served as first deputy.

title: 2030 Winter Games: Historic French fort will be turned into an Olympic village
subhed: The developer Linkcity, partnering with architect Philippe Prost, will renovate the Fort Trois-Têtes in Briançon, which is to host between 900 and 1,200 athletes.

title: 'Lyhanna was a victim twice': Women-led rallies across France condemn failures after schoolgirl's death
subhed: Several thousand people gathered on Monday in front of courthouses and city halls to express their dismay at the judicial treatment of sexual violence committed against children. Many demonstrators called for the resignation of Justice Minister Gérald Darmanin.

title: France revives Gratitude Train for the US's 250th anniversary
subhed: A restored railcar from northern France will retrace the journey of the Gratitude Train, which carried gifts from Le Havre to New York in 1949.

title: Women's desire, a men's issue?
subhed: No, women should not feel pressured to boost their libidos when they wane, writes sex columnist Maïa Mazaurette, who urges both men and women to reconsider female desire from a societal perspective.
